name: Minnesota (United States)

colors.symbolism: evokes the night sky and Minnesota's shape
the abundance of water and the bright skies of Minnesota

description: A dark navy 'K'-shaped hoist (echoing the geographic outline of Minnesota with its prominent Northwest Angle) bears a white eight-pointed star at its centre. The fly is a solid light-blue field.
history: Designed by Andrew Prekker and selected by the State Emblems Redesign Commission established in 2023; adopted on 19 December 2023 and made the official state flag on 11 May 2024 (Statehood Day), replacing the 1957 seal-on-bedsheet flag. The redesign followed criticism that the previous flag's central seal depicted a settler ploughing while a Native American rode away — imagery widely seen as harmful — and that the design was visually indistinguishable at distance from many other U.S. state flags.

features.symbolism: the hoist a dark navy 'K' shape echoing Minnesota's geographic outline; the fly a solid light-blue field representing the state's lakes and waters

features.role: white eight-pointed North Star on the navy hoist
features.symbolism: the North Star, alluding to the state motto 'L'Étoile du Nord' ('The Star of the North'); the eight points evoke unity and the cardinal and ordinal directions
